item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love broken English, really. It's my comedical weakness, and it's really well done here, you should be proud. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
given that the products are "often harmful to the general public," mr gock must be quite the naughty boy to be begged to continue making this stuff rather than the alternative…

I'll readily admit that I'm easy to please, but this had my upvote at "am the libertarian." of course the presumably genocidal eldritch abomination is an ancap. i'm shocked, shocked I tell you! actually, I've had conversations online with ancaps that don't really deviate too far from this. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Regaards,
P. Hudson Gock CEO of Totleigh Software yes

+1 for that, -1 for making another Factory/Doctor Wondertainment clone
Congratulations, Dave.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In The Leak, it's said that Soft Geigh (it's what I call him) was an old colleague of another eldritch entity.

Perhaps he was a shop-floor Demon of Hell.

item.title: Junior Staff Post - Crosslink Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Timer expired - edit implemented.
The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ Proposed Edits
- Proposed Edits

The proposed edit is to link the article to the MTF hub page entry for Hammer Down. The article references the MTF in its containment procedures.

Editing this:

Area-2803 is to be supplied with rotating armed personnel from MTF Nu-7 "Hammer Down" in the event of SCP-2803-A entering an active state.

To this:

Area-2803 is to be supplied with rotating armed personnel from MTF Nu-7 "Hammer Down" in the event of SCP-2803-A entering an active state.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
God, that interview at the end is fantastic. It’s a perfect use of the “[DATA EXPUNGED]” brackets to really turn something funny into something creepy without losing any of the comedic edge in the process. It’s easy to go overboard, using the “data expunged” device as a crutch and losing a lot of potential detail, but this really strikes that perfect “Pulp Fiction briefcase” level of ambiguity for me. In this case, the lack of elaboration strikes me as genuinely beneficial to the piece, and legitimately clever.

Hilarious, eerie and not too hokey in any particular area. Great skip. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly the scariest part of this is imagining what the hell that thing was doing before that would make a Foundation researcher actually beg it to keep doing what it is now. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the space entity coming to live the American dream

Hmm, brings a literal look at the aliens who go up to live the American dream, eh?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Argh, this one is frustrating to me. It feels like it's almost there. I like the space entity coming to live the American dream, but the interview feels shallow and overall the whole thing feels like it was a missed opportunity to make something great. Instead it's kind of flat and not really as engaging as it could be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's an annoying median for me. It tells too much for it to remain mysterious but doesn't tell enough for it to feel complete.

I still love it, though. My favorite part is a formless eldritch abomination from beyond the stars somehow knowing about the american dream.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
am the libertrarian

…annnd upvoted. god bless our space-destroyer free-market tycoon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If one views this as its own piece, rather than a part of a series, it's excellent. It reminds me quite a bit of Bogleech's work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A humorous and enjoyable read!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it ;c 1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought we agreed never to speak of that again

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
well,
it doesn't say this is TotleighSoft's only building

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for that. I'll be honest, I have a tendency to be extremely hard on myself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It isn't an embarrassment. If you want an embarrassment, you should've been there when I [REDACTED] and it [REDACTED] ended up drenched in [DATA EXPUNGED] and Mariah Carey.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh? That's a little like Laurel & Hardy being down with themselves for only doing slapstick.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dave, my comment was intended as an observation, but absolutely not as censorship. And I don't consider this article and embarrassment (as you've suggested elsewhere) - as I said, there is some good technique in evidence here. It's just not my preferred style.

And who the hell cares about what I think? Write what you want to write, what makes you happy, at the end of the day.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Attempting a major overhaul. Something that might make this article a little less of an embarrassment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like both previous TotleighSoft SCPs, but not this one. Posters above summed up my thoughts on the matter, pretty much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Every time a Randomini appears, a GoI gets its wings.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This actually is kind of freaking me out. Not because it's harsh - I appreciate you telling me all of this. But from the sudden realization that all my stuff is pretty much the same. It's hard to take in, and I've got no one to blame but myself. Maybe I should make less articles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll be honest - posting this was a tricky decision. Some people thought that, with my previous two articles, I didn't specify enough about TotleighSoft. Now, I've specified too much. Truth be told, this was the explanation I've had in mind the whole time, and I'm keeping it up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This sums it up basically. This is why classifying GOIs as SCPs almost always leads to unsatisfying results. You basically force an in-universe explanation on the other SCPs that can't be ignored, and it is one that isn't really all that great. Even out-of-context, this article is dull and predictable, leading to a downvote regardless, where the other two skips could be teetering on upvotes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm going to agree with what psul said, and add this: this explanation is basically the least satisfying explanation that could be offered, even more unsatisfying, I'd argue, than never answering the question at all.

I wasn't a big fan of the original video game articles, but I at least thought that there were intriguing questions to be had from them. Why would a modern gaming company emulate an Assyrian god? Why does it resemble bootleg software? Is there a better version out there?

The answer to every question, this article shows, is "haha lol no, an alien did it because he's stupid". Any hint of intriguing "why" the previous articles evoked is just answered with "because he's bad at making video games, lol".

I will grant this article a few things: the description of the entity is rather nice, and the foundation pleading for it to continue making video games is somewhat cute. But beyond that, this article obliterates the only things, in my view, the previous articles had going for them, and psul's comment sadly made me realize how formulaic this all is. There isn't a deeper story because the formula barely allows for a basic story.

So, all in all: downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In retrospect, while I sincerely enjoyed the base idea and interview, TotleighSoft as an SCP felt like a bad move. I feel that it ultimately restricts Massy, PORRIDGE and other SCPs that could be attributed to it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While the article itself is not very substantial, the Foundation begging this thing to keep making anomalous software, as opposed to whatever it was doing before it came to earth, just tickled me the right way. Maybe I'm easy to please, but I liked it enough that I think it should stick around too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Or was it actually… DIO!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's a limit to how much comedically misspelled, Spanko-style interviews I can enjoy, and I think I've reached it. It feels as though there are two dave-templates operating at the moment, one of which finishes with a short interview log which establishes a single character trait, usually for comedic effect (this one, 2324, 4055-J, 2527, 3V1L-J, TTKU-J, SCP-J) and the other of which uses escalating lists for comedic effect (WTF-J, 7000-J). 2219 and 2416 combine both templates. Many of the shorter-lived SCPs also fall into one of these camps.

That's not to say that the templates aren't effective. They are, and there are some good writing techniques at work in each of them. And they clearly work for the up-voting audience. It's just become a bit predictable for me - there's only so many times I can see the same trick done well and still be impressed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Shouldn't that be mentioned as a forenote in the interview or something? It makes it seems like the Foundation chose Facebook as the method of communication.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Personally, I feel like a group like TotleighSoft should remain mysterious. I prefer them as some shadowy, unknowable organization where I'm free to come up with my own interpretations. Having the mystery unraveled here wasn't very satisfying for me, to be honest. It's a decent read, but I just wasn't feeling it. Neutral vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Facebook was what 2803-A was using when it made first contact.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Would the Foundation even use facebook messaging, I mean, facebook isn't exactly the most secure site on the internet… (I might just be missing something though) Other than that it didn't really grab me, probably just the idea itself didn't get me, I think the main thing about this that doesn't sit well with me is as thekillerax described below, ie it no longer being shadowy and more of an unknown mysterious organization. no vote from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ehhhhhhh…. Same thing as what I literally just posted in Massy's discussion page. This feels incomplete.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nope, wasn't you.
This is evidence.

12:28TL333s I KNEW I'd seen Totleigh Soft somewhere before
12:28 *** Randomini joined #site19
12:28 TL333s time for a new GoI!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love it. TotleighSoft more like Totleigh Awesome!

no known method to dampen or neutralize the lambda waves

Someone needs their Telekiiiiiill…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: D'awwww!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Except it was me. >:|

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That someone *might* have been me…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Someone on Site19 briefly mentioned that Totleigh should get progressed to become a GOI. A few minutes later, this comes up.

Also just as good as previous skips. Stop being so fast :| +1
